Xzibit © 2025 · Internal Use Only
BRAND
SYSTEM
Internal Operational Reference — v2.0
2025 · Confidential · Not for external distribution
01 — Brand Foundation

WHAT WE DO
& WHO WE ARE

What Xzibit Does

Xzibit designs, fabricates and delivers immersive environments for museums, exhibitions and experiential spaces. We operate end-to-end — from initial concept through to installation — across four service pillars: Displays, Spaces, Experiences and Stories.

Brand Personality
  • Confident — direct, decisive, no hedging language
  • Practical — solutions-focused, not theoretical
  • Creative — visually ambitious, story-driven
  • Technical — precise, credible, detail-oriented
  • Collaborative — partner-focused, not transactional
Tagline — Exact Format
imagination.captured
  • Always lowercase — no exceptions
  • Dot separator, no space: imagination.captured
  • Never: Imagination Captured / IMAGINATION.CAPTURED
DSES Descriptor
D S E S
  • D — Displays
  • S — Spaces
  • E — Experiences
  • S — Stories
02 — Non-Negotiable Rules

RULES &
CONSTRAINTS

These rules apply to all Xzibit outputs — proposals, case studies, presentations, social media, and marketing collateral. No exceptions without written approval from the Creative Lead.

Headings
Word Count
  • Page / section title: MAX 4 WORDS
  • Subheading: MAX 7 WORDS
  • Caption / label: MAX 10 WORDS
  • No full sentences as headings
Case Style
  • Page titles: ALL CAPS
  • Section headings: ALL CAPS
  • Subheadings: Title Case
  • Body text: Sentence case
  • Labels / tags: ALL CAPS
Punctuation
  • No full stops in headings
  • No exclamation marks — ever
  • Em dash (—) for pauses, not hyphen
  • No ellipsis (...) in headings
  • Ampersand (&) permitted in headings
Body Text
Word Count Limits
  • Synopsis / intro: 40–60 WORDS
  • Feature bullet: MAX 6 WORDS
  • Caption: MAX 15 WORDS
  • Full paragraph: MAX 80 WORDS
  • Proposal page body: MAX 120 WORDS
Voice Rules
  • Active voice only — always
  • Subject performs the action
  • ✓ "Xzibit designed and built..."
  • ✗ "The project was designed by..."
  • Present tense for capabilities
  • Past tense for completed projects
Banned Phrases NEVER USE
  • was contracted to
  • the project required
  • we were tasked with
  • solutions provider
  • world-class / best-in-class
  • leveraging / utilising
  • stakeholders
Images
Layout Coverage
  • Project pages: MIN 70% VISUAL
  • Hero image: FULL WIDTH
  • Max images per page: 5
  • Min 1 hero image per project
  • No images below 1200px wide
Acceptable Types
  • Finished environment photography
  • Detail / craft shots
  • In-use / visitor experience shots
  • Renders (clearly labelled)
  • NO STOCK PHOTOGRAPHY for projects
  • NO UNFINISHED SPACES
Image Quality
  • Minimum resolution: 300 DPI PRINT
  • Minimum resolution: 72 DPI SCREEN
  • Colour grade: cool, dark, cinematic
  • No warm/orange grading
  • No heavy filters or effects
  • No watermarks or logos from others
Layout
Spacing System
  • Base unit: 8PX
  • Component padding: 16PX / 24PX / 32PX
  • Section padding: 64PX / 80PX
  • Gutter between columns: 16PX OR 24PX
  • Never use arbitrary values (e.g. 13px, 22px)
Grid & Alignment
  • 12-column grid for print layouts
  • Max content width: 1280PX
  • Left-align all body text
  • Left-align all headings (no centred headings)
  • No orphaned single words on last line
  • Consistent column gutters throughout
03 — Logo System

LOGO
RULES

Full Mark + DSES — Reversed
Use on #1D252D only PRIMARY
Full Mark + DSES — Standard
Use on white / light backgrounds
Wordmark Only — Reversed
Secondary use, no descriptor
XZ Mark Only
Favicon / icon / embossed use
Clearspace Rule
  • Minimum clearspace = height of "X" mark
  • Apply on all four sides
  • No text, imagery or graphics inside clearspace
  • Digital min width: 120PX
  • Print min width: 30MM
  • Mark only min: 24PX / 8MM
Approved Backgrounds
  • Reversed logo: #1D252D only
  • Standard logo: white or off-white only
  • Mark only: any solid dark or teal
  • Never on mid-grey (#888A8B)
  • Never on photography without overlay
  • Never on patterned backgrounds
Absolute Don'ts NEVER
  • Do not recolour the logo
  • Do not rotate or flip
  • Do not stretch or distort
  • Do not add drop shadows or effects
  • Do not recreate in any other font
  • Do not use low-resolution versions
Logo Don'ts — Visual Reference
✗ Recoloured
✗ Mirrored
✗ Rotated
✗ Distorted
✗ On Mid-Grey
✗ Drop Shadow
04 — Colour Palette

COLOUR
RULES

Copy HEX
Brand Teal PRIMARY ACCENT
Pantone 3262 CP HEX #19B1A1 RGB 25 · 177 · 161 CMYK 81 · 0 · 40 · 0
Copy HEX
Dark Charcoal PRIMARY BG
Pantone 433 C HEX #1D252D RGB 29 · 37 · 45 CMYK 80 · 69 · 58 · 67
Copy HEX
Cool Grey SUPPORTING
Pantone Cool Gray 8 CP HEX #888A8B RGB 136 · 138 · 139 CMYK 23 · 16 · 13 · 46
Teal Usage Rules
  • Use for: accents, icons, labels, dividers, CTAs
  • Max teal coverage per page: 20%
  • Never use teal as a body text colour
  • Never use teal on teal
  • Hover state: #0D8A7E (Teal Dark)
Dark Charcoal Usage
  • Primary background for premium outputs
  • Always pair with reversed (white/teal) logo
  • Body text on dark: rgba(255,255,255,0.7)
  • Headings on dark: #FFFFFF
  • Never use pure black (#000000)
Forbidden Combinations
  • Teal text on teal background
  • Grey text on white (insufficient contrast)
  • Any colour not in this palette
  • Gradients using brand colours
  • Pure black (#000000) backgrounds
05 — Typography

TYPE
SYSTEM

Hurme Geometric Sans 1 is the only approved typeface. Use the correct weight for each role — never substitute, never mix in other fonts.

Display
Black / 900
72–96px / 48–64pt
Line height: 1.0
Tracking: −1px
IMAGINATION
Hero headlines, section titles, large-scale typographic moments. Always uppercase.
Heading H1
Bold / 700
36–48px / 24–32pt
Line height: 1.1
Tracking: 0
PROJECT TITLE
Page headings, proposal section titles. Uppercase.
Heading H2
SemiBold / 600
20–28px / 14–18pt
Line height: 1.2
Tracking: +0.5px
SECTION SUBHEADING
Subheadings, card titles, category labels.
Body
Regular / 400
13–15px / 9–11pt
Line height: 1.7
Tracking: 0
We design and build immersive environments that bring stories to life — from concept through to installation.
Body copy, descriptions, proposal text. Sentence case.
Caption
Light / 300
10–12px / 7–9pt
Line height: 1.6
Tracking: +0.5px
Mon Repos Turtle Centre — Queensland, Australia. Completed 2023.
Image captions, footnotes, metadata.
Label / Tag
SemiBold / 600
9–11px / 6–8pt
Line height: 1.4
Tracking: +2.5px
MUSEUMS & EXPERIENCE CENTRES
Navigation, tags, badges, section labels. Always uppercase, wide tracking.
Typography Don'ts NEVER
  • Any font other than Hurme Geometric Sans 1
  • Centred body text
  • Justified text
  • Italic style (not in brand typeface)
  • Underlined text (use bold for emphasis)
  • Text smaller than 9px / 6pt
Web Fallback Stack
  • Primary: Hurme Geometric Sans 1 (WOFF2)
  • Fallback 1: League Spartan (Google Fonts)
  • Fallback 2: system-ui
  • Fallback 3: sans-serif
  • CSS: 'HurmeGeometric', 'League Spartan', system-ui, sans-serif
06 — Case Study Template

LOCKED
TEMPLATE

Every case study must follow this exact structure. No additional sections. No reordering. No exceptions.

FieldRuleNotes
Project NameMax 4 words. Title Case.Use the venue name, not a descriptor. ✓ "Mon Repos Turtle Centre" ✗ "Immersive Wildlife Experience Project"
ClientExact legal or trading name.No abbreviations unless the client uses them.
Synopsis40–60 words. Must include: client, challenge, outcome.Active voice. No passive constructions. One paragraph only.
Features3–5 bullet points. Max 6 words each.Start each with a strong verb. ✓ "Custom-fabricated fossil display cases" ✗ "The project included the fabrication of custom fossil display cases"
Scope IconsSelect from fixed categories only (see below).Do not create new categories.
PhotographyMin 1 hero image (full width). Max 4 supporting images.Real project photography only. No renders unless labelled.
Fixed Scope Categories
🏗
Fabrication
🎨
Design
📺
AV Integration
💡
Lighting
🖨
Large Format Print
🖐
Interactive
📦
Modular Systems
🚚
Install & Logistics
Formatted Example
Mon Repos Turtle Centre
Client: Queensland Parks & Wildlife Service
Synopsis
Queensland Parks and Wildlife Service engaged Xzibit to transform the Mon Repos Visitor Centre into a world-leading turtle conservation experience. Xzibit delivered a fully immersive environment that educates visitors on loggerhead turtle biology and conservation, increasing visitor engagement and dwell time by over 40%.
Features
  • Custom-fabricated turtle life-cycle displays
  • Immersive projection mapping environment
  • Interactive touch-screen interpretation panels
  • Large-format environmental photography prints
  • Full AV integration and control systems
Design Fabrication AV Integration Large Format Print Interactive Install & Logistics
07 — Do / Don't Examples

RIGHT VS
WRONG

Headlines — Length & Strength
✓ Do — Strong, ShortTURTLE CENTRE
4 words. Uppercase. No punctuation.
✗ Don't — Weak, LongAn Immersive Experience Exploring the Life and Conservation of the Loggerhead Turtle at Mon Repos
Too long. Sentence case. Reads as body copy.
✓ Do — Active, SpecificIMMERSIVE FOSSIL DISCOVERY
Specific, visual, impactful.
✗ Don't — VagueA GREAT PROJECT WE COMPLETED!
Exclamation mark. Vague. No substance.
Body Copy — Active vs Passive
✓ Do — Active VoiceXzibit designed and fabricated a fully immersive fossil discovery environment for the Riversleigh Fossil Discovery Centre, delivering the project on time and under budget.
✗ Don't — Passive VoiceXzibit was contracted to provide design and fabrication services. The project required the delivery of an immersive fossil discovery environment. The project was completed successfully.
✓ Do — Concise FeatureCustom-fabricated fossil display cases
6 words. Starts with strong descriptor.
✗ Don't — Verbose FeatureThe project included the design and fabrication of a series of custom fossil display cases to house the collection
21 words. Passive. Wordy.
Imagery — Hero vs Cluttered
✓ Do — Strong Hero
One full-width hero image
70%+ of layout
✗ Don't — Too Many Small
8 small images. No visual impact. Cluttered.
Layout — Clean vs Cluttered
✓ Do — Clean Layout
Large image. Clear hierarchy. Generous space.
✗ Don't — Cluttered
No hierarchy. Cramped. No breathing room.
08 — Layout System

LAYOUT
RULES

Spacing Scale
8px
Icon padding, tight component gaps
16px
Component internal padding, small gaps
24px
Card padding, medium gaps between elements
32px
Section sub-spacing, between content blocks
48px
Between major content sections
64px
Section padding (top/bottom)
80px
Large section padding, hero margins
Approved Layout Variations
70 / 30 Split
Primary layout. Image left, text right. Use for case studies and project pages.
Full-Width Hero
Hero image top (60%), 2-column text below. Use for section openers.
50 / 50 Split
Equal columns. Use for comparisons, team pages, or two-image layouts.
Image-to-Text Ratios
  • Project / case study page: MIN 70% IMAGE
  • Capability / service page: MIN 50% IMAGE
  • About / team page: MIN 40% IMAGE
  • Contact / info page: MIN 20% IMAGE
  • Never text-only pages in proposals
Alignment Rules
  • All text: left-aligned
  • No centred body text — ever
  • Logo: always top-left in documents
  • Page numbers: bottom-right
  • Consistent left margin: 40PX / 15MM
  • Consistent right margin: 40PX / 15MM
09 — File & Asset Management

FILE
STANDARDS

File Naming Convention
File TypeFormatExample
Project files XZIBIT_[ClientCode]_[ProjectName]_[Type]_v[#] XZIBIT_QPWS_MonRepos_Proposal_v3.indd
Images [ClientCode]_[ProjectName]_[Description]_[###] QPWS_MonRepos_TurtleDisplay_001.jpg
Presentations XZIBIT_[ClientCode]_[Type]_[YYYYMMDD]_v[#] XZIBIT_BAES_Capabilities_20250315_v2.pptx
Logos (outgoing) Xzibit_Logo_[Variant]_[ColourMode] Xzibit_Logo_FullMark_Reversed.svg
Templates XZIBIT_Template_[Name]_v[#] XZIBIT_Template_CaseStudy_v2.indd
Naming Rules
  • No spaces — use underscores only
  • No special characters: / \ : * ? " < > |
  • Dates: YYYYMMDD FORMAT
  • Version: v1, v2, v3 — not "final", "final2", "FINAL"
  • Client codes: 3–6 uppercase letters
Version Control
  • Start at v1 — never v0 or "draft"
  • Increment on every client-facing send
  • Keep all versions — never overwrite
  • Archive superseded versions to /Archive subfolder
  • Final approved: add _APPROVED suffix
Server Folder Structure
📁 XZIBIT_Projects/
  📁 [ClientCode]_[ClientName]/
    📁 [ProjectName]/
      📁 01_Brief/
      📁 02_Design/
      📁 03_Proposals/
      📁 04_Production/
      📁 05_Photography/
        📁 RAW/
        📁 Edited/
        📁 Approved/
      📁 06_Correspondence/
      📁 Archive/

📁 XZIBIT_Brand/
  📁 Logos/  → SVG, AI, EPS, PNG variants
  📁 Fonts/  → OTF, WOFF2 files
  📁 Templates/  → INDD, PPTX, DOCX
  📁 Guidelines/  → This document
10 — Final Checklist

PRE-SEND
CHECKLIST

Complete this checklist before finalising any client-facing document, presentation or proposal. All items must be checked. No exceptions.

Content & Copy
  • Headlines follow rules Max 4 words · Uppercase · No punctuation · No exclamation marks
  • Word counts are correct Synopsis: 40–60 words · Features: max 6 words each · Body: max 120 words per page
  • Active voice throughout No "was contracted to", "the project required", "was tasked with"
  • No banned phrases used Check against banned list in Section 02
  • Tagline formatted correctly imagination.captured — lowercase, dot separator, no space
Visual & Layout
  • Images meet requirements Min 70% visual on project pages · Min 1200px wide · No stock photography
  • Correct logo version used Reversed on dark · Standard on light · Correct clearspace maintained
  • Correct colours used Only #19B1A1, #1D252D, #888A8B and supporting palette
  • Typography is correct Hurme Geometric Sans 1 only · Correct weights · No centred text
  • Layout follows grid Spacing in 8px increments · Left-aligned · Consistent margins
Files & Delivery
  • File named correctly XZIBIT_[ClientCode]_[Name]_[Type]_v[#] · No spaces · Correct date format
  • Saved in correct location Correct project folder on server · Correct subfolder (Design/Proposals/etc.)
  • Version number is correct Incremented from last version · Previous version moved to /Archive
  • Export format is correct Client-facing: PDF · Editable: INDD/PPTX · Images: JPG 300dpi or PNG
  • Proofread by second person Not the author · Check spelling, client name, dates, figures
